posts.raw_content:
This site currently requires that we be here to support MAGA and Trump. 

Supporting MAGA is a given. Requiring Trump support made complete sense when this website was vital in national elections and Trump was the candidate. That's done now, he got his two (three) terms. 

Why require it now? 

It's becoming increasingly clear that this administration and Trump personally are overly influenced by special interests that too often diverge from genuine America First principles - which is what most of us thought MAGA was about.

To be clear, I still support Trump, but pretty lukewarmly. He's, obviously, much better than the alternative and I do not regret my votes and support. I will continue to defend him.

But I'm also disappointed. Epstein files, slowrolled and backtracked deportations, lack of action on election fraud, lack of action against the deep state and other internal enemies, etc. The more-war-for-Israel thing isn't something I'm thrilled about either. I'm not as against it as many here, I don't particularly mind as long as we don't end up in something protracted with ground forces committed, I'd just rather see us not do the bidding of foreign powers with so many of our own problems remaining unsolved. Perhaps part of it is that Trump's scope for domestic action has been constrained by the treasonous GOP. But still. 

It's clear that we need a lot better in the future. He's probably the best we could have hoped for 2017-2029. He's the first stepping stone to securing our sovereignty but, obviously, not the end goal. 

At some point we have to let go of Trump. And this site at some point needs to transition. 2028 is coming. We need a more America First candidate. And we need to be able to talk critically.

If anyone looks through major threads, Israel First shills are downvoted to oblivion and people critical of Trump right now get upvoted. So it's clear that many of us are already 'violating' the 'be here to support Trump' rule.

I'm not suggesting that we turn *against* Trump. I'm saying that qualified support and open criticism is needed going forward.

posts.raw_content:
Hi everyone,

Just to let those who care know I'm having a double mastectomy this Thursday. I've been quite emotional about all of this but have accepted it is going to be my new normal. This will be my 3rd new normal to adjust to. The first was losing my baby girl at 25 weeks. She was born alive but died shortly after. I was able to become pregnant again not long after and so adjusting to that loss, while very difficult at the time, was nothing compared to the brain aneurysm rupture and 40 days in ICU. I left there with brain damage and had to adjust to a new normal. A normal in which I could no longer do my job, no longer multi task, adjust to memory issues and find new ways to deal with planning and organizing. I made it to the other side and found normal again.

Now I'm losing my breasts. It's ok though bc these breasts have cancer in them and the cancer is trying to kill me. So I'm going to get this done. Look at my new body sometime next week and start accepting this new body minus breasts. It's ok. They accomplished their main purpose when I nursed my girls and they did it well. Now, they have to go. 

I've been on a medication break for the last few weeks which has resolved some of the pain and fatigue I was having. I've developed breathing issues as a result of that med and now have to use an inhaler. When I go back on meds it will be something different and hopefully I won't be dealing with the joint pain and extreme fatigue. 

I want to thank all of you that have prayed for me and ask for continued prayers as I undertake this new path in life. I'm praying for peace of mind and a return to some measure of my former self where I can be active and enjoy life again. 

I'll probably post another update in a few weeks after I've recovered from this surgery just to say I'm alive. 

Until then...
